title: It Is And It Isn’t: On John Carpenter, Schrödinger’s Cat, and Assault on Precinct 13
description: It’s not just the idea that the station = a box that’s compelling me to draw these maybe obtuse, maybe overly-academic, maybe seemingly random connections. John Carpenter has built these ideas of superposition and coexisting states into the very thematic structure of Assault on Precinct 13.
hook: John Carpenter's Assault on Precinct 13 uses quantum mechanics as its actual plot structure—a film about coexisting realities disguised as a siege movie.

title: Book Review: ‘Presence: A Hidden History of the Female Body,’ by Erin Maglaque
description: In “Presence,” the historian Erin Maglaque pieces together the fragments of early modern womanhood.
hook: A historian reconstructs early modern women's lives from fragments—what it meant to have a body when almost nothing was written down about it.

title: “Seize the Means of Knowledge Production”: Durba Mitra on Third World Feminists and Their Fight Against Patriarchal Authoritarianism
description:
“I suggest that the building of women’s organizations through cross border collaboration leads to a total transformation of civil society.”

The post “Seize the Means of Knowledge Production”: Durba Mitra on Third World Feminists and Their Fight Against Patriarchal Authoritarianism appeared first on
hook: Third World feminists built knowledge networks across borders that transformed how women organized against authoritarianism—a history we've mostly forgotten.

title: Natural Disaster by Lisa Owens review – the last day of maternity leave is a comic rollercoaster
description:
Parenting is represented in all its hilarious, moving and truthfully plodding detail, in the story of a mother and her two little boys

The last day of maternity leave, and an unnamed mother of two decides to stage a “yes day”, full of treats and good feelings. Of course it does not go according to plan: the treats are deficient, misjudged and underappreciated; the good feelings are fleeting, quickly upstaged by anxiety, guilt or humiliation. This familiar-sounding scenario is the simple
hook: One mother's last day of maternity leave becomes a comic catastrophe that captures the plodding, hilarious violence of early parenting.

title: Blu-ray Review: Lisa Cholodenko’s Debut Feature ‘High Art’ on the Criterion Collection
description:
Criterion has outfitted Cholodenko’s breakthrough debut with a good-looking transfer.

The post Blu-ray Review: Lisa Cholodenko’s Debut Feature ‘High Art’ on the Criterion Collection appeared
hook: Lisa Cholodenko's High Art on Criterion—the debut that built a career out of photographing female desire with unnerving clarity.
